name: Altomira Trail
name_original: Sierra de Altomira — circular desde Saceda-Trasierra
description: Moderate 6.6-mile circular hike in Sierra de Altomira, offering panoramic views and unique geological features from Saceda-Trasierra.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Alcarria traverse a diverse landscape shaped by its plateau origins and significant Karst phenomena. The region features deep river valleys, dramatic gorges, and distinctive karst formations carved by rivers like the Tagus and Guadiela. Hikers can explore a variety of terrains, from open fields to areas with unique geological structures and hills. Best easy hiking trails around Alcarria The most popular easy hiking route is Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route offers views of the Escaba river and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra Gorda Trail, an easy 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This trail winds through varied natural scenery, including gentle ascents and descents. Local hikers also love the Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras, a 1.0 mile (1.6 km) trail leading through unique carved rock formations, often completed in about 25 minutes. Hiking around Alcarria is defined by river valleys, gorges, karst landscapes, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 37 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Alcarria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcarria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Alcarria?
A: Alcarria offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with &lt;strong&gt;37 routes&lt;/strong&gt; specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are 87 hiking routes across all difficulty levels in the region.

Q: What are some of the shortest easy hikes in Alcarria?
A: For a quick and easy walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt;, which is only 1.0 mile (1.6 km) and takes about 25 minutes. Another short option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.1-mile (3.4 km) trail that can be completed in about an hour.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in Alcarria?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Alcarria are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt; is a loop, offering views of the Escaba river. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a circular route.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes in Alcarria?
A: Alcarria's easy hikes often traverse diverse landscapes shaped by its plateau origins and significant karst phenomena. You can expect to see deep river valleys, dramatic gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt;, and unique karst formations. The region is also known for its rolling hills and, during summer, vibrant lavender fields, particularly around Brihuega and parts of Alcarria of Cuenca.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes in Alcarria?
A: Many of Alcarria's easy trails are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt; is a short, engaging walk through unique carved rock formations that children often enjoy. Trails around natural pools, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares&lt;/a&gt;, also offer pleasant experiences for all ages.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I find near easy hiking trails?
A: Near many easy trails, you can explore unique attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; features fascinating rock carvings. You might also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas. The region also has historical sites and charming villages, such as Brihuega, known for its medieval character.

Q: When is the best time to visit Alcarria for easy hiking?
A: The best time for easy hiking in Alcarria depends on your preferences. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. However, if you wish to experience the vibrant lavender fields, late June to mid-July is ideal, when the region transforms with blooming violet hues. Be aware that summer can be hot, so plan early morning or late afternoon hikes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in Alcarria?
A: The easy trails in Alcarria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied natural scenery, the gentle ascents and descents, and the unique geological formations like the carved rocks of the Route of the Faces. The presence of river valleys and natural pools also adds to the appeal.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer views of the 'Tetas de Viana'?
A: While specific easy trails directly ascend the 'Tetas de Viana' (Teta Redonda has a rustic staircase), many routes in the broader Alcarria region will offer distant views of these distinctive twin hills, which are a prominent feature on the horizon. Look for trails with open vistas to catch a glimpse of them.

Q: Can I find easy hikes that pass by marsh landscapes or dams?
A: Yes, Alcarria features spectacular marsh landscapes and dams. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a notable attraction in the region. While specific easy routes passing directly over the dam might vary, many trails in the surrounding areas will offer views or access to these water features and their associated forests.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for dog walking in Alcarria?
A: Many easy trails in Alcarria are suitable for walking with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you follow local regulations. Trails that wind through varied natural scenery, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242091539/sierra-gorda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Gorda Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer open spaces and natural environments for you and your pet to enjoy. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hiking trails in Alcarria?
A: Many popular trailheads for easy hikes in Alcarria, such as the starting point for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop&lt;/a&gt;, have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific tour details on komoot for information on parking availability at your chosen trailhead.
tips: The place where the hermitage is located is the highest point of the Sierra de Altomira, which stands out over the territory. On clear days you can enjoy the exceptional views of La Alcarria, northern La Mancha and the Meseta Madrileña. Next to the hermitage is the geodesic vertex of the Altomira peak (1183 m) and, a little further north, the ruins of an old tower located in a watchtower.
Nice path through the Sierra de Altomira, a terrain of little difficulty.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Sierra de Altomira — circular desde Saceda-Trasierra trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hiking route typically takes between 3.5 to 3.8 hours to complete. The total distance is approximately 11 kilometers (6.8 miles).

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered to be of easy to moderate difficulty. While some sections are straightforward, the main challenge involves a gradual ascent towards the Sierra de Altomira, which includes some zig-zag sections on rocky terrain. It's a good option for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra de Altomira — circular desde Saceda-Trasierra trail start?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in the charming village of Saceda-Trasierra, following the Camino del Calvario. You can find parking in the village, making it convenient to start your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Altomira — circular desde Saceda-Trasierra trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a protected natural area, dogs are generally permitted. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the diverse wildlife and the presence of livestock in the surrounding areas. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to hike this trail, as the landscape becomes vibrant with color and natural aromas from countless aromatic plants. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, and winters can be chilly but offer a different kind of beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in Sierra de Altomira?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and hike this trail in the Sierra de Altomira. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Sierra de Altomira — circular desde Saceda-Trasierra route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter the unique limestone sinkhole known as the Hoyo Redondo early in the hike. The highest point, Pico Altomira, offers spectacular 360-degree panoramic views of the Alcarria region and distant mountain ranges. At the summit, you'll also find the Hermitage of the Virgin of Altomira. The trail itself is part of the Sierra de Altomira Trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The path is a combination of trails and tracks. While some sections are smooth, others become rockier or more uneven, particularly during the climb to the summit of the sierra. You'll navigate through Mediterranean forest landscapes.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Altomira is a protected natural area with rich biodiversity. You might spot various birds of prey like royal eagles, booted eagles, and griffon vultures. The area is also home to mustelids and ungulates. The Mediterranean forest features pine trees, holm oaks, gall oaks, and a variety of aromatic low scrub, including endemic species like Antirrhinum microphyllum.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is also known as PR-CU 40 Camino de Altomira, and it utilizes sections of the official Camino de Altomira trail for approximately 67% of its length.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to Saceda-Trasierra?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Saceda-Trasierra may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance if you plan to rely on public transport. Driving and parking in the village is generally the most convenient option for hikers.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Altomira
